content.text: How can I enforce documentation standards and check for missing docstrings across various Python code structures?
If a method has a very short docstring (one line), will this function correctly flag it if the configured minimum length is two lines?

The logic uses `max_lines` from the configuration. If the difference between the last and first line number (`lines`) is less than this maximum allowed length, the check returns, effectively skipping the report.
Thanks for clarifying how the docstring lengths are evaluated!
